item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Having to read the comments to "get" the article secures an instant downvote from me. I'm sorry

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, but I had to wade through far too much mindfuck for much too small a payoff - the execution is fine, it's just that the concept is inherently obfuscated.

I don't really see an easy way to improve this, so the figurative neutral vote applies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this is the most crosslinking main SCP article ever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well… makes sense. I'm used to saving my progress at a crystal already. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One thing I didn't see mentioned here (although it's not the reason for my downvote, said reason being that the payoff:buildup ratio is far too small) is bog-standard relativistic time dilation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like I've already seen a similar concept, and the copious crosslinks are making my head spin. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Basically, they're save files for reality. When someone unleashes a major fuckup, like an XK scenario, you can use the crystal to restart the universe at the save point.

And lately (if that word has any meaning in this context), a lot of stuff has been going to hell, possibly literally, causing a lot of resets…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really liked the draft, really like the article. This fits in perfectly within the Xyank canon, which I guess isn't a technical canon but I enjoy it anyway.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think I've managed to wrap my head around this one:

So we have these little windows to "snapshots" of our universe, frozen at the exact time of their creation, popping into existence on a regular basis. Because they never change, we can use them as reference points - hard back-ups, if you will - to effectively un-fuck our timeframe after a large-scale time-bending anomaly. When one breaks, our whole universe is reset to the time represented by that little octahedron - and that's already happened at least once, so our timeframe has been wound back X minutes earlier than it should be (which we only know about thanks to 2400). Now the order of these manifestations has been seriously messed up and we're getting more and more objects from the "future" piling up, threatening to overwhelm us.

I dunno, that seemed like a lot of mental wrangling to get to a conclusion that wasn't particularly satisfying. Decent writing, though. No vote for now.

item.title: 1d8
item.content:encoded:
Crosslinking is a thing.
Will make a collab log soon if this sticks.
This'll be my last SCP before the 3000 contest (because of my obsession with using the numbers 2, 3, 4 and 9) unless a slot opens up.

And uhh… soy noodles are tasty.
